name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: AbbVie’s repeated price increases on Humira have had cost our health care system billions of dollars, and they are simply unsustainable going forward.  We need real structural reforms, like #HR3, if we are going to bring any relief to patients.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: In fact, the net price increased by 110 percent—more than doubling—between 2009 and 2018.    Dr. Kesselheim, what does this drastic increase in net price say to you about the role, if any, of rebates in driving up Humira’s price over time?

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: That means that a year’s supply of Humira now costs over $77,000 dollars.   Internal data show that Humira’s net price⸺or the list price minus all rebates, discounts, and fees⸺has also increased over time.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: Today, the list price of a single 40-milligram syringe of Humira is a $2,984⸺470 percent more than its price at launch.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: You’ll notice that price increases really ramped up after 2013⸺the year AbbVie spun off, and the year you became CEO.   AbbVie has raised the price of Humira 14 times in just the past 8 years.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: Since that time, the increases in price for Humira have only accelerated.  I’d like to show a graph showing how the price of a 40-millgram syringe of Humira has grown from 2003 to today.  https://t.co/oztg86WiTE

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: Mr. Gonzalez, that’s when you became the CEO, correct?

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: By 2013, a single 40-milligram syringe of the drug was priced at $1,024⸺nearly double what it was ten years earlier.  AbbVie, spun off as its own company in 2013, took Humira with it.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: In 2003, @Abbott_Labs launched Humira at a price of $522 per 40-milligram syringe, or just over $6,200 annually.  Over the course of the next decade, Abbott raised the price of Humira 13 times.

tweet: Or it can live up to our nation’s founding principles, join the 54% of Americans—and growing—who support #DCStatehood and pass #HR51.  I yield back.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: Congress has a choice. It can continue to exclude DC residents from the democratic process, forcing them to watch from the sidelines as Congress votes on federal and #DC laws, and to treat them, in the words of Frederick Douglass, as “aliens, not citizens, but subjects.”

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: DC's gross domestic product is larger than 17 states. In 2016, 86 percent of DC residents voted for statehood.   DC residents have been petitioning for voting representation in Congress and local autonomy for 220 years.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: DC’s population of 712,000 is larger than two states.   #DC pays more federal taxes per capita than any state and pays more federal taxes than 21 states.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: The District Clause gives Congress plenary authority over the federal district and establishes a maximum size of the federal district—100 square miles. It does not establish a minimum size or a location of the federal district.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: The Admissions Clause gives Congress the authority to admit new states.   All 37 new states were admitted by Congress.   No state was admitted by constitutional amendment, and no state would have to consent to the admission of the State of Washington, DC.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: This country was founded on the principles of no taxation without representation and consent of the governed, but #DC residents are taxed without representation and cannot consent to the laws under which they, as American citizens, must live.

tweet: Richard Holmes made it as far as DC, a walk to freedom but not to equal citizenship so far for our family.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: For me, this is deeply personal. My own family has lived in D.C. since my great-grandfather Richard Holmes, as a slave, walked away from a plantation in Virginia and made his way to DC almost 200 years ago.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: The citizens who live in our nation’s capital have never had voting representation on the floor of either chamber of Congress, and Congress has always had the final say on their local affairs.   This is uniquely un-American and undemocratic.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: For the 220 years since DC became the capital of the United States, DC residents, who have always had all of the obligations of citizenship, including paying full federal taxes and serving in the armed forces, have been excluded from much of American democracy.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: Last Congress, when the House passed the #DCStatehood bill, it was the first time in history either chamber of Congress had passed it.   This Congress, with Democrats controlling the House, Senate and @WhiteHouse, DC statehood is within reach for the first time in history.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: Or it can live up to the nation’s promise and ideals, end taxation without representation and pass #DCStatehood.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: Congress can continue to exercise autocratic authority over the American citizens who reside in our nation’s capital, treating them, in the words of Frederick Douglass, the inspiration for the name of the new state, as “aliens, not citizens, but subjects.”

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: DC servicemembers have helped get voting rights for people throughout the world, yet have always been denied those same rights when they returned home.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: As American citizens, DC residents are entitled to equal citizenship, but they have also earned it. They have fought in every American war, including the war that led to the creation of the nation, the Revolutionary War.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: My own family has lived in DC since my great-grandfather Richard Holmes, as a slave, walked away from a plantation in Virginia and made his way to DC almost 200 years ago.  Richard Holmes made it as far as DC, a walk to freedom but not to equal citizenship so far for our family.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: DC pays more federal taxes per capita than any state and pays more federal taxes than 21 states.   DC has a gross domestic product larger than 17 states.   DC's bond rating is higher than the rating of 32 states.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: DC's population is larger than two states, and the State of Washington, DC would be one of seven states with a population under one million.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: Although #DC does have car dealerships, notably, the Framers left car dealerships out of the Admissions Clause, and, until last month’s hearing on #DCStatehood, the number of car dealerships had never been raised as an element in admission decisions.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: Congress generally has considered three elements in admission decisions: population and resources, support for statehood and commitment to democracy.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: These two days remind us that D.C. residents are taxed without voting representation in Congress and are denied equal political rights.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: Today's #DCStatehood markup could not be more timely.  Tomorrow is the traditional federal tax filing deadline, and Friday is Emancipation Day, which celebrates the freeing of 3,100 enslaved persons in D.C. in 1862.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: #DCStatehood has both the Constitution and facts on its side.   The Constitution’s Admissions Clause gives Congress the authority to admit new states, but a new state may not be created from an existing state without the existing state’s consent.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: America was founded on the principles of no taxation without representation and consent of the governed, but the United States is the only democratic country that denies voting representation in the national legislature to the residents of the capital.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: Congress has both the constitutional authority and moral obligation to pass #DCStatehood.  The Constitution gives Congress the authority to admit new states, which it has done 37 times, and to reduce the size of the federal district, which it did by 30 percent in 1846.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: There are hundreds of thousands of #DC residents who see nobody except their local #USPS letter carriers.   Their work is very much felt by the people I represent.  Thank you for coming today.  #ForThePeople #WeAreDC

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: But even if it was planned, who in the world, in the middle of a pandemic when these sorting machines were most desperately needed, would proceed to take them out now?  #USPSSabotage

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: #DeJoy is saying that some of this, taking the sorting machines out, was planned.   At our @OversightDems hearing Monday we will get him to show us that was actually the case.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: The problem at the moment is the delivery of mail.   In fact the mail was being delivered, but we have had the intervention of #PostmasterGeneralDeJoy to stop it.   Unacceptable.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: "Help. Help. Help."   -- #ANCCommissioner Carmon of #Ward8 on the problems with #USPS deliveries experienced lately.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: This delivery problem is being felt in our communities, and yet we have not heard their voices. While I've heard from people across the city, I've heard the most consistent essential delivery problems from my constituents in #Ward8.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: I'm grateful to the #ANCCommissioners coming forward today, Commissioner Carmon of Ward 8D and Commissioner Adofo of Ward 8C, and to the two postal worker union representatives who joined us today, Mr. Robinson from the @APWUnational and Mr. Hooks from @NALC_National.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: But what is needed now is an understanding of what #PostmasterGeneralDeJoy's interference means on the ground.   We won't be able to fix what has gone wrong with #USPS until we hear from those most affected.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: We can still prevent an indelible scar on our free elections that will result from changes @USPS. We must pass the #DeliveringforAmericaAct and the $25 billion needed to save the #USPS.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: As far as I am concerned, the job is still to be done.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: #DeJoy has admitted that the harm done so far will be left in place -- no replacement of sorting machines or of blue mailboxes that have been removed, and no promise for adequate #USPS overtime.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: We've heard from almost everyone except the people most directly affected by the crisis at the #USPS.   Missing from the discussion are voices of the people on the #frontlines, our elected #ANCcommissioners, and our #PostalWorkers, who hold the bulk of the burden @USPS is facing.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: The House passed our #DCStatehood bill in June.   The Senate must stop denying our 705,000 federal taxpaying residents congressional voting rights and full local self-government.  #DCStatehood now.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: An act of Congress admitted all 37 new states.  There is no constitutional, financial, or historical reason Congress can’t or shouldn’t make DC a state.   #DCStatehood now.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: DC pays more federal taxes per capita than any state and more total federal taxes than 22 states.   The U.S. sends DC residents to war but denies them congressional voting rights and full local self-government.   #DCStatehood now.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: The United States is the only democratic country that denies the residents of the nation’s capital *both* voting rights in the national legislature and full local self-government.  #DCStatehood now.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: Wyoming has 578,000 residents and three voting members of the House and Senate.   Vermont has 623,000 residents and three voting members of the House and Senate.   D.C. has 705,000 residents and zero voting members of the House or Senate.   #DCStatehood now.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: The mission during the remainder of this Congress is clear. We need to educate the American public about #DCStatehood, and we need to build support for the D.C. statehood bill in the Senate. Today’s @SenateDems hearing is a great start.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: The Senate can continue to exercise undemocratic authority over the citizens who reside in our nation’s capital, treating them, in the words of #FrederickDouglass, as “aliens, not citizens, but subjects.” Or it can live up to our nation’s promise and ideals and pass #DCStatehood.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: #DCStatehood is deeply personal for me. My great-grandfather Richard Holmes, who escaped as a slave from a Virginia plantation, made it to D.C., a walk to freedom but not to equality. For three generations my family has been denied the rights other Americans take for granted.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: The United States is the only democratic country that denies both voting rights in the national legislature and local autonomy to the residents of its nation’s capital. The Senate has both the constitutional authority and moral obligation to end this injustice. #DCStatehood

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: In a few days, Americans will celebrate Independence Day. However, the nation’s oldest slogan, “no taxation without representation,” and the principle of consent of the governed still do not apply to the Americans living in the nation’s capital. #DCStatehood

tweet: My great-grandfather Richard Holmes, a slave, escaped a plantation in Virginia to reach the District of Columbia.  He did a walk to freedom, but not one to equal citizenship.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: Coming in this, the third century of our nation, #DCStatehood means much more to us than dollars and cents. Statehood is priceless. Statehood assures that living in our nation’s capital is about pride, not prejudice.

name: Eleanor #DCStatehood Holmes Norton
tweet: For the first time, #DCStatehood will put an end to our oldest slogan: “taxation without representation.” To crown that denial, D.C. residents pay the highest federal taxes per capita, without equal representation.

tweet: The #DCStatehood bill will always have a personal acknowledgement from me in the name of Richard Holmes, my great-grandfather, who escaped as a slave from a Virginia plantation. He made it as far as the District of Columbia, a walk to freedom but not to equal citizenship.
